message: I am unable to mark tasks as complete, how do I do this?
answer:
To mark tasks as complete in Workfront, follow these steps:

1. Click the **Menu** icon in the top-right corner, then select **Work List**. You can also use the menu to navigate to parent objects.
2. From the **Work List**, select the task or issue you wish to mark complete.
3. Click **Done**. If you are the only user assigned to this task or issue, this action will mark the item as Complete or Resolved in Workfront.
4. If multiple users are assigned to this task or issue, clicking Done will open a dropdown. You can then choose:
   - **Done with my part** if other users still need to complete their work for the task or issue to be fully complete.
   - **Complete** (for tasks) or **Resolved** (for issues) if no further work is needed on the task or issue. This will mark the item as Complete or Resolved in Workfront.

Please note that if the work item has predecessors, the Done button will be gray until those predecessors are marked complete. You can view predecessors by clicking on the task name.

Sources:
- [Mark work items complete using the Adobe Workfront plugin: Complete a work...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/adobe-workfront-integrations/workfront-for-creative-cloud/manage-wf-cc/wf-cc-complete#:~:text=Complete%20a%20work%20item1%20Click%20the%20Menu,6b-2202-40d6-bcd0-3ee0c2316b43)

message: How do I input PTO?
answer:
To input Paid Time Off (PTO) in Workfront, you typically need to follow these steps:

1. **Navigate to the Time Tracking Section**: Go to the Time Tracking area within your Workfront interface. This is usually found in the main menu.

2. **Select the Appropriate Date Range**: Choose the date range for which you want to input your PTO. This may involve selecting a specific week or month.

3. **Add Time Off**: Look for an option to add time off or PTO. This might be labeled as "Add PTO" or "Request Time Off."

4. **Fill in the Details**: Enter the necessary details, such as the type of leave (PTO), the start and end dates, and any additional notes if required.

5. **Submit for Approval**: After filling in the details, submit your PTO request for approval. Depending on your organization's settings, this may need to be approved by a manager or HR.

6. **Check Status**: You can check the status of your PTO request in the same section to see if it has been approved or if any further action is needed.

If you encounter any issues or need specific guidance based on your organization's setup, it may be helpful to reach out to your Workfront administrator or HR department for assistance.

message: What is 'Pin current Page' and how does it make Workfront easier to use?
answer:
'Pin current page' is a feature in Workfront that allows users to pin their most important work items, such as projects, tasks, or request queues, for quick and easy access. When you pin a page, it becomes accessible from the top of any page within Workfront, enhancing visibility and organization.

Here’s how it makes Workfront easier to use:

1. **Increased Visibility**: By pinning important pages, you can quickly locate and access them without having to navigate through multiple menus or search for them each time.

2. **Better Organization**: Pinned pages help you keep your workspace organized by allowing you to prioritize the items that matter most to you. This can streamline your workflow and improve efficiency.

3. **Quick Access**: Since pinned pages are always available at the top of the interface, you can switch between different work items seamlessly, saving time and effort.

To pin a page, follow these steps:

1. Navigate to the page you want to pin.
2. Click on **Pin current page** at the top of the page.

Once pinned, your page will be displayed alongside any predefined pins that may be set by your layout template, providing a customized workspace tailored to your needs.

Sources:
- [Pin pages to customize your workspace: Pin a page1...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/basics/navigate/pin-pages#:~:text=Pin%20a%20page1%20Go%20to%20the%20page%20you%20want%20to,from%20the%20top%20of%20any%20page%20in%20Workfront)

message: How do  I make a custom form field be required for specific statuses for taks or issues
answer:
To make a custom form field required for specific statuses for tasks or issues in Workfront, you will need to follow these steps:

1. **Access the Custom Form**: Click on the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner of Adobe Workfront, then navigate to **Setup**.

2. **Locate Custom Forms**: In the left panel, find and click on **Custom Forms**. This will display a list of existing custom forms.

3. **Edit the Custom Form**: Select the custom form you want to modify. Click the **Edit** icon next to the form.

4. **Add or Modify Fields**: If you need to add a new field, click on **Add Field**. If you are modifying an existing field, select it from the list.

5. **Set Field as Required**: In the field settings, look for an option that allows you to set the field as required. This may be labeled as "Required" or similar.

6. **Configure Status Conditions**: To make the field required only for specific statuses, you will need to set conditions based on the status. This typically involves using a conditional logic feature where you can specify that the field is required when certain statuses are selected.

7. **Save Changes**: Once you have configured the field and its conditions, make sure to save your changes to the custom form.

8. **Test the Configuration**: After saving, test the custom form by creating or editing a task or issue to ensure that the field behaves as expected based on the selected status.

By following these steps, you can effectively make a custom form field required for specific statuses in Workfront.

Sources:

message: What is the conditional logic that would do this?
answer:
To set up conditional logic that makes a custom form field required for the 'Complete' status in Workfront, you will typically follow these steps:

1. **Access the Custom Form**: Start by navigating to the **Main Menu** in Workfront and selecting **Setup**.

2. **Locate Custom Forms**: In the left panel, click on **Custom Forms** to view the list of existing forms.

3. **Edit the Custom Form**: Find the custom form you want to modify and click the **Edit** icon next to it.

4. **Select the Field**: Choose the field you want to make required, in this case, the 'Page/Block ID' field.

5. **Set Conditional Logic**: Look for an option to add conditional logic. You will need to specify that the field is required when the status is set to 'Complete'. The logic might look something like this:
   - **If** Status **is equal to** 'Complete' (with the key 'CPL'), **then** 'Page/Block ID' **is required**.

6. **Save Changes**: After configuring the conditional logic, ensure you save your changes to the custom form.

7. **Test the Configuration**: Finally, create or edit a task or issue to verify that the 'Page/Block ID' field is indeed required when the status is set to 'Complete'.

By following these steps, you can effectively implement the conditional logic needed to make the custom form field required for the 'Complete' status in Workfront.

Based on the uploaded notes, the top 3 business objectives for WestJet are:

1. **Incorporate Online Guest Behavioral Data into Customer 360 Profile**: WestJet aims to integrate their online guest behavioral data into a comprehensive customer 360 profile within their Snowflake environment. This involves stitching the data to deanonymize it and make it useful for creating a holistic view of their customers.

2. **Expedite Data Integration Process**: WestJet wants to accelerate the process of incorporating their online data into the customer 360 profile, rather than waiting until mid-2026 for the full migration to AEP/CJA. They are considering interim solutions to achieve this goal much sooner.

3. **Evaluate and Optimize Data Export and Stitching Methods**: WestJet is exploring various options to export and stitch their data effectively. This includes exporting raw, non-stitched data from Adobe Analytics, leveraging the AA data connector to quickly port data to AEP, and using Query Service or CJA for stitching and exporting the data. They are also seeking to understand the cost implications and data volume requirements for these options.

message: What are the top 3 pain points?
answer: Are you referring to the file uploaded or the screen you are on?

message: The Uploaded File
answer:
Please wait - This process can take a while depending on the size of your document... 

The top 3 pain points are:

1. **Inability to Export Stitched Data**: WestJet's current Adobe Analytics (AA) setup does not allow for the export of pre-stitched data via the Data Feeds functionality, which is necessary for their integration needs with Snowflake.

2. **Long Migration Timeline**: The progressive migration to Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) and Customer Journey Analytics (CJA) is expected to take until mid-2026, which is too long for WestJet to wait to incorporate their online data into their customer 360 profile.

3. **Manual Stitching Requirement**: Due to the inability to export stitched data, WestJet is considering the labor-intensive process of exporting raw, non-stitched data and manually stitching it outside of the Adobe ecosystem, which adds complexity and potential for errors.

message: What does Success look like?
answer: Are you referring to the file uploaded or the screen you are on?

message: The Uploaded File
answer:
Please wait - This process can take a while depending on the size of your document... 

Success for WestJet in this context would involve the following outcomes:

1. **Incorporation of Online Guest Behavioral Data**: Successfully integrating online guest behavioral data into the customer 360 profile within the Snowflake environment.

2. **Data Stitching and Deanonymization**: Achieving the ability to stitch and deanonymize the data using the same field-based stitching method historically used, despite the current limitations with Adobe Analytics (AA) and Cross-Device Analytics (CDA).

3. **Timely Data Export**: Exporting the necessary raw, hit-level data from AA in a non-stitched format and manually stitching it outside the Adobe ecosystem, or quickly porting data over to Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) using the AA data connector.

4. **Interim Solutions**: Implementing one of the interim options to support the overarching use case much sooner than the mid-2026 timeline for full migration to AEP/CJA:
   - Exporting existing AA data via Data Feeds and manually stitching it.
   - Using the AA data connector to port data to AEP and then using CJA or Query Service for stitching and exporting.

5. **Cost and Volume Estimation**: Accurately estimating the number of rows of data generated and determining if the existing licensed volume will support the proposed actions, or calculating the incremental cost if additional volume is needed.

6. **Seamless Transition**: Ensuring that the data ported to AEP can live separately from the ongoing Web SDK migrations and associated CJA connections/data views, allowing for a smooth transition and integration process.

Overall, success would mean WestJet can efficiently and effectively integrate their online guest behavioral data into their customer 360 profile, enabling better customer insights and decision-making without having to wait for the full migration to AEP/CJA.

message: How to create a report of all the projects which are running behind their schedule
answer:
To create a report of all the projects that are running behind schedule in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. **Access the Reports Section**: Navigate to the Reports area from the main menu in Workfront.

2. **Create a New Report**: Click on the "Create Report" button to start a new report.

3. **Select Report Type**: Choose the type of report you want to create. For tracking projects, a list report is typically a good starting point.

4. **Set Filters**: In the Filters tab, you will need to set specific criteria to identify projects that are behind schedule. You can add filters such as:
   - **Project Status**: Set this to "Late" or "At Risk" to capture projects that are not on track.
   - **Task Progress Status**: You may also want to filter by task progress to ensure you are only looking at tasks that are incomplete and contributing to the project's delay.

5. **Group by Project Name**: In the Groupings tab, group the report by project name to see all tasks associated with each project clearly.

6. **Save the Report**: Once you have configured the filters and groupings, save the report with a relevant name, such as "Projects Running Behind Schedule."

7. **Review and Edit**: After saving, you can review the report results. If needed, you can continue editing the report to refine the data displayed.

8. **Add Visuals (Optional)**: If you want to visualize the data, you can add a chart to the report. A pie chart can be useful to show the distribution of projects that are behind schedule.

By following these steps, you will be able to create a comprehensive report that highlights all projects currently running behind their schedule.

message: What are the differences between program and project
answer:
In Workfront, the terms "program" and "project" refer to different levels of organization and management of work. Here are the key differences:

1. **Definition**: A project is a specific work item that consists of a group of tasks aimed at achieving a particular goal. It is a standalone effort with defined objectives, timelines, and resources. In contrast, a program is a collection of related projects managed in a coordinated manner to obtain benefits and control not available from managing them individually. Programs often focus on broader organizational goals and strategic alignment.

2. **Scope**: Projects have a narrower scope, focusing on specific deliverables and outcomes. Programs encompass a wider scope, integrating multiple projects to achieve overarching objectives.

3. **Management**: Projects are typically managed by project managers who oversee the execution of tasks and ensure that the project stays on track. Programs are managed by program managers who coordinate between projects, ensuring that they align with the strategic goals of the organization and that resources are allocated effectively across projects.

4. **Duration**: Projects usually have a defined start and end date, while programs may be ongoing and can evolve over time as new projects are added or existing ones are modified.

5. **Benefits Realization**: The success of a project is measured by its ability to meet its specific goals within the constraints of time, budget, and quality. The success of a program is measured by the overall benefits it delivers to the organization, which may include improved efficiency, increased revenue, or enhanced customer satisfaction.

Understanding these differences can help in effectively managing work within Workfront, ensuring that both projects and programs are aligned with organizational goals.

Sources:
- [What's new in API version 17: Project (PROJ)Projects are...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/adobe-workfront-api/api-notes/new-api-version-17#:~:text=Project%20%28PROJ%29Projects%20are%20work,removed%20the%20flag%20INLINE_EDITABLE)

message: What does Task "Planned Hours" mean?
answer:
In Workfront, "Planned Hours" refer to the estimated amount of time required to complete a specific task. These estimates are crucial for effective resource management, as they help identify whether team members are over-allocated or under-allocated based on the tasks assigned to them.

Here are some key points about Planned Hours:

1. **Estimation**: Planned Hours are essentially your best guess on how long a task will take. If the Planned Hours for a task are less than the actual duration, it indicates that the person assigned to the task has the capacity to take on additional work.

2. **Resource Management**: Workfront utilizes Planned Hours to show resource managers the allocation of their team members. This visibility helps in managing workloads effectively.

3. **Time Tracking**: Tracking the actual time spent on tasks against the Planned Hours can improve future estimates. This practice allows for better planning in subsequent projects.

4. **Clarifying Expectations**: Planned Hours also serve as a communication tool between managers and team members. If a team member disagrees with the estimated hours, it opens up a dialogue to clarify the scope of work.

5. **Work Effort Feature**: If you want to use Planned Hours but do not require high accuracy, you might consider using Workfront’s Work Effort feature, which allows for more flexible time estimates.

Understanding and utilizing Planned Hours effectively can enhance project planning and execution within Workfront.

Sources:
- [Understand planned hours: DocumentationWorkfrontUndersta,in the text...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront-learn/tutorials-workfront/manage-work/tasks/understand-planned-hours#:~:text=DocumentationWorkfrontUndersta,in%20the%20text%20after%20this%20video)

message: Can I export Workfront Planning data?
answer:
Yes, you can export Workfront Planning data. To do this, follow these steps:

1. Click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner of Adobe Workfront, or if available, click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-left corner, then select **Resourcing**. The **Planner** will display by default.
2. Choose the view for the Planner. You can select one of the following options:
   - View by User
   - View by Project
   - View by Role
3. Click **Export**. This will open the Export Options dialog box.
4. Specify the following information:
   - **Start Date**: Enter the start date for your export. The exported file will include allocation and availability information starting from the first day of the week that contains this date.
   - **Number of Periods**: Indicate how many time periods you want to include in your file. The default is 4 periods.
   - **Type**: Choose the type of time periods for the exported file (weeks, months, or quarters). The maximum time periods you can export are:
     - 52 weeks
     - 36 months
     - 12 quarters
5. Select what to export based on the view you selected:
   - In the Project View, you can choose to export Projects, Projects and Roles, or Everything (default).
   - In the User View, you can choose to export Users, Users and Projects, or Everything (default).
   - In the Role View, you can choose to export Roles, Roles and Projects, or Everything (default).

By following these steps, you will be able to successfully export the planning data you need from Workfront.

Sources:
- [Export Information from the Resource Planner: Export information from...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/manage-resources/resource-planning-in-adobe-workfront/export-resource-planner#:~:text=Export%20information%20from%20the,%28this%20is%20the%20default%20option%29)

message: How to setup cross-project predecessor
answer:
To set up a cross-project predecessor in Workfront, follow these steps:

1. **Open the Project**: Navigate to the project where you want to establish the predecessor relationship.

2. **Access the Tasks**: Go to the Tasks tab within the project.

3. **Select the Task**: Identify the task that you want to set as a predecessor.

4. **Edit Task Details**: Click on the task to open its details. Look for the option to add dependencies.

5. **Add Dependency**: In the dependency section, you can specify the predecessor task from another project. You may need to search for the task by name or ID.

6. **Save Changes**: After selecting the predecessor task, ensure you save the changes to establish the relationship.

7. **Verify the Dependency**: Check the task details to confirm that the predecessor has been correctly set up.

By following these steps, you can effectively create a cross-project predecessor relationship, allowing for better project management and scheduling.

Sources:

message: how about for when a project is completed, can you have a predecessor on project level instead of task
answer:
To set up a predecessor relationship at the project level in Workfront after a project is completed, you can follow these steps:

1. **Open the Completed Project**: Navigate to the project that has been completed and where you want to establish the predecessor relationship.

2. **Access the Project Details**: Click on the project to open its details. 

3. **Identify the Task**: Within the project, identify the task that you want to set as a predecessor for another task in a different project.

4. **Edit Task Details**: Click on the task to open its details. Look for the option to add dependencies.

5. **Add Dependency**: In the dependency section, you can specify the task from another project that you want to set as a successor. You may need to search for the task by name or ID.

6. **Specify Dependency Type**: Choose the type of dependency you want to establish (e.g., Finish-Start).

7. **Save Changes**: After selecting the predecessor task, ensure you save the changes to establish the relationship.

8. **Verify the Dependency**: Check the task details to confirm that the predecessor has been correctly set up.

By following these steps, you can effectively create a predecessor relationship at the project level, even after the project has been completed. This can help in managing future projects that depend on the outcomes of the completed project.

message: how do i create a new user?
answer:
To create a new user in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. Click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner of Adobe Workfront, or if available, click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-left corner, then select **Users**.
2. Choose the user that you want to copy, then click the Copy icon.
3. In the **Copy User** box that appears, edit the fields available for the new user as needed.
4. Click **Add This Person** to create the new user. Alternatively, you can click **Add Person & Start Another** if you want to save the new user and add another one.

This process will create a new account in Workfront for the user. If you selected the option to send an invitation to the user, they will receive an email with a link to create their Workfront password. 

Please note that if your organization has been onboarded to the Admin Console and you add a user through Workfront, you will not have the option to send an email invitation. Additionally, for existing Adobe users, whether they receive an email about Workfront being available is controlled by the Adobe administrator for the product.

message: i would like to create a report that shows hours by week for my team for the past 8 months.  how do i do that?
answer:
To create a report in Workfront that shows hours by week for your team over the past 8 months, you can follow these steps:

1. **Access the Reports Section**: Click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner, or the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-left corner, if available, then click **Reports**.

2. **Create a New Report**: Click **New Report** and select **More** > **Hour Report**.

3. **Set Up Filters**: 
   - Go to the **Filters** tab.
   - Set a filter to show hours reported as having been worked during the last 8 months. You can use the entry date to filter the hours.
   - Additionally, you may want to filter by the home team if you want to focus on your specific team.

4. **Configure Groupings**:
   - Navigate to the **Groupings** tab.
   - Select **Switch to Matrix Grouping** to create a matrix report.
   - For **Row Grouping**, choose **Owner Name** to display hours worked by each team member.
   - For **Column Groupings**, select the hour entry date by month, and then add a secondary column grouping of hour entry date by week.

5. **Adjust Columns**:
   - Go to the **Columns** tab.
   - Ensure that the **Hours** column is set to summarize by **Sum** so that the total hours will appear in the matrix.

6. **Save Your Report**: Once you have configured the report to your liking, click **Save + Close** to save your report.

message: how do I grant someone access to the AI assistant
answer:
To grant someone access to the AI Assistant in Workfront, you need to ensure that certain requirements are met. Here are the steps to follow:

1. **Check Access Requirements**: 
   - Ensure that your organization has a signed Adobe Gen AI agreement on file. If not, the AI Assistant cannot be enabled.
   - Confirm that the user has the appropriate Adobe Workfront plan and license. The plan can be any for new users, but for current users, it is not available. The license must be Standard for new users, and current users cannot have it available.
   - The user must also have the necessary access level configurations, meaning you must be a Workfront administrator to grant access.

2. **Enable AI Assistant**:
   - As a Workfront Administrator, click the AI Assistant icon.
   - If the Adobe Gen AI agreement has not been signed, you will see a message prompting you to review the agreement. Follow the instructions to enter the name and email address of the individual who will sign the agreement.
   - Once the agreement is signed and returned, the AI Assistant will be enabled for your organization, allowing you to grant access to other users.

3. **Inform Users**: 
   - After enabling the AI Assistant, inform the users that they can now access it. They can start using it by clicking on the AI Assistant icon in their Workfront interface.

By following these steps, you can successfully grant someone access to the AI Assistant in Workfront.

message: how do I turn off a report daily email notification?
answer:
To turn off a report daily email notification in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. Click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner of Adobe Workfront.
2. Click your user name next to your profile picture, or if available, click your profile picture in the top navigation area and then select **Workfront Profile**.
3. Click the **More** icon next to your name, then click **Edit**.
4. In the **Edit Person** box that appears, navigate to the **Notifications** section.
5. Look for the category related to report notifications. Click on that category to view the specific notification settings.
6. Deselect the check box next to the daily email notification you want to turn off. If there is an option to deactivate all notifications in that category, you can use that as well.

After making your changes, ensure that you save your settings. This will stop the daily email notifications for the report.

message: What’s the largest file size I can upload in proof?
answer:
In Workfront, the largest file size you can upload as a proof is subject to specific limits based on the file type. Here are the key limits to keep in mind:

1. **General File Size Limit**: Files must be less than 4 GB per file.
2. **Microsoft Word and PowerPoint Files**: These files (DOC, DOCX, PPT, PPTX) must be less than 512 MB.
3. **Excel Files**: XLS and XLSX files must be less than 100 MB.
4. **Files Attached to Comments on Proofs**: These must be less than 30 MB.
5. **PNG Files**: The height and width must be less than 65535 pixels.
6. **Static Combined Proofs**: You can support up to 50 files in a single proof.
7. **Documents Uploaded as Proofs**: These must be less than 2,000 pages.

It's important to note that larger files may take more time to upload, and if your internet connection is unstable, the upload process may be interrupted. 

If you have any specific file types in mind or need assistance with the upload process, feel free to ask!

Sources:
- [Supported proofing file types and size limits overview: File size limitsWorkfront...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/review-and-approve-work/proofing/proofing-overview/supported-proofing-file-types#:~:text=File%20size%20limitsWorkfront%20has%20the,action%20may%20be%20terminated%20or%20broken)

The campaign is scheduled to run from January 1, 2023, to December 31, 2023, with awareness and consideration programs planned for each quarter across North America, EMEA (Europe, Middle East, and Africa), and APAC (Asia-Pacific) regions.

message: What is 'Pin current Page' and how does it make Workfront easier to use?
answer:
'Pin current page' is a feature in Workfront that allows users to pin their most important work items, such as projects, tasks, or request queues, for quick and easy access. When you pin a page, it becomes accessible from the top of any page within Workfront, enhancing visibility and organization.

Here’s how it makes Workfront easier to use:

1. **Increased Visibility**: By pinning important pages, you can quickly locate and access them without having to navigate through multiple menus or search for them each time.

2. **Better Organization**: Pinned pages help you keep your workspace organized by allowing you to prioritize the items that matter most to you. This can streamline your workflow and improve efficiency.

message: can I pull waterfall projects into a kanban board?
answer:
In Workfront, while the Kanban methodology is primarily designed for agile project management, it is possible to incorporate elements of waterfall projects into a Kanban board. However, the integration may not be straightforward since waterfall projects typically follow a linear, sequential approach, whereas Kanban focuses on continuous flow and flexibility.

To pull waterfall projects into a Kanban board, you can follow these steps:

1. **Identify Key Deliverables**: Break down your waterfall project into key deliverables or phases that can be represented as individual tasks or stories on the Kanban board.

2. **Create a Kanban Board**: If you haven't already, create a Kanban board in Workfront. You can do this by navigating to the appropriate section in your Workfront interface and selecting the option to create a new Kanban board.

3. **Add Tasks to the Board**: Manually add the identified deliverables or tasks from your waterfall project to the Kanban board. You can create new tasks or stories that reflect the stages of your waterfall project.

4. **Set Up Work In Progress (WIP) Limits**: If applicable, configure WIP limits on your Kanban board to manage the flow of work and ensure that your team is not overloaded.

5. **Monitor Progress**: Use the Kanban board to track the progress of your waterfall project. You can move tasks across the board as they progress through different stages.

While this approach allows you to visualize waterfall projects in a Kanban format, keep in mind that the inherent differences between the two methodologies may require some adjustments in how you manage and track your projects.

Sources:
- [Create an Agile Team: Benefits of using...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/agile/agile-in-workfront/create-an-agile-team#:~:text=Benefits%20of%20using%20Kanban%20in,%28WIP%29%20limit%20on%20theKanban%20board)

message: What are some error handlers I should consider using if creating a simple scenario that creates an update in Workfront based on an update to a task?
answer:
When creating a simple scenario in Workfront that updates based on a task update, it's essential to implement effective error handling to ensure smooth execution. Here are some error handlers you should consider:

1. **Error Handling Directives**: Utilize error handling directives to manage potential issues that may arise during the task update process. For instance, you can set up a directive to log an error message or update a record with a note indicating that an error occurred during the task creation.

2. **Success and Warning Labels**: Be aware that in the execution history, actions will be labeled as a success, a warning, or an error. This labeling helps you quickly identify the outcome of each action and take appropriate measures if something goes wrong.

3. **Creating Alternative Paths**: Design your scenario to include alternative paths for when errors do not occur. This allows you to continue processing other tasks or updates even if one part of the scenario fails.

4. **Manual Resolution of Errors**: Consider implementing a mechanism to store incomplete executions. This way, you can revisit and resolve errors manually if needed, ensuring that no updates are lost.

By incorporating these error handlers, you can create a more robust and reliable scenario in Workfront that effectively manages updates based on task changes.

message: How do I attach a file
answer:
To attach a file in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. Navigate to the specific card where you want to add the attachment.
2. Look for the option to **Add an Attachment**. This action module allows you to attach files to the selected card.
3. You will need to enter the **Card ID**. You can do this in two ways:
   - **Enter manually**: In the **Card ID** field, enter or map the ID of the card you want to add an attachment to.
   - **Select**: Choose the board that contains the card, then select the list that contains the card, and finally select the card itself.
4. Choose the **Attachment type**:
   - **File**: Select a source file from a previous module or map the source file's name and data.
   - **URL**: Enter the URL to the file and provide a name for the attachment.
5. Once you have selected the file or entered the URL, confirm the action to complete the attachment process.

This will successfully attach your file to the specified card in Workfront.

Sources:
- [Trello modules: CardsAdd an Attachment...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront-fusion/using/references/apps-and-their-modules/third-party-app-connectors/trello-modules#:~:text=CardsAdd%20an%20Attachment%20%20This%20action,creates%20a%20card%20in%20a%20selected%20list)

message: What is 'Pin current Page' and how does it make Workfront easier to use?
answer:
'Pin current Page' is a feature in Workfront that allows users to pin their most important work pages for quick access and better organization. When you pin a page, it becomes easily accessible from the top of any page within Workfront, ensuring that you can quickly navigate to the resources or tasks that matter most to you.

This feature enhances the usability of Workfront in several ways:

1. **Increased Visibility**: By pinning important pages, you can keep critical information at your fingertips, reducing the time spent searching for frequently used resources.

2. **Better Organization**: Pinned pages help you maintain a tidy workspace by allowing you to prioritize what you need to see and access regularly.

3. **Quick Access**: With pinned pages always visible, you can switch between different tasks or projects seamlessly, improving your workflow efficiency.

To pin a page, you typically would look for a 'Pin current Page' option in the interface, which allows you to add the current page to your pinned list. However, if you are assigned to a layout template that has predefined pins, those cannot be renamed or removed, and any pages you pin will display to the right of these predefined pins.

message: How can you check in a user is a light user or a standard user?
answer:
To determine if a user is classified as a light user or a standard user in Workfront, you typically need to check the user's license type or role settings within the system. Here are the steps you can follow:

1. **Access User Management**: Navigate to the User Management section in Workfront. This is usually found under the Admin area.

2. **Locate the User**: Search for the specific user you want to check. You can use the search bar or scroll through the list of users.

3. **Check User Details**: Click on the user's name to open their profile. Here, you should find information regarding their license type.

4. **Identify License Type**: Look for a field labeled "License Type" or "User Role." This will indicate whether the user is a light user or a standard user. Light users typically have limited access and capabilities compared to standard users.

5. **Review Permissions**: If needed, you can also review the permissions associated with each user role to understand the differences in access and functionality.

If you have any specific questions about user roles or need further assistance with user management, feel free to ask!

message: What is the largest file size I can upload in proof
answer:
In Workfront, the largest file size you can upload as a proof is determined by the type of file you are working with. Here are the specific limits:

1. **General File Size Limit**: Files must be less than 4 GB per file.
2. **Microsoft Word and PowerPoint Files**: These files (DOC, DOCX, PPT, PPTX) must be less than 512 MB.
3. **Excel Files**: XLS and XLSX files must be less than 100 MB.
4. **Files Attached to Comments on Proofs**: These must be less than 30 MB.
5. **PNG Files**: The height and width must be less than 65535 pixels.
6. **Static Combined Proofs**: You can support up to 50 files in a single proof.
7. **Documents Uploaded as Proofs**: These must be less than 2,000 pages.

It's important to note that larger files may take more time to upload, and if your internet connection is unstable, the upload process may be interrupted.

Sources:
- [Supported proofing file types and size limits overview: File size limitsWorkfront...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/review-and-approve-work/proofing/proofing-overview/supported-proofing-file-types#:~:text=File%20size%20limitsWorkfront%20has%20the,action%20may%20be%20terminated%20or%20broken)

message: how can i parse csv to checkbox field

1. **Use the Parse CSV Transformer**: Start by utilizing the Parse CSV transformer, which allows you to parse CSV text from a received text value or a file.

2. **Specify the Number of Columns**: In the configuration of the Parse CSV transformer, specify the number of columns that your CSV file contains.

3. **Indicate if CSV Contains Headers**: If your CSV file has headers in the first row, select the option that indicates this. This will ensure that the headers are not included in the output data.

4. **Select the Delimiter Type**: Choose the appropriate delimiter for your CSV file. Common options include Comma, Tab, or you can specify another character if needed.

5. **Preserve Quotes**: If your CSV data includes quotes that you want to preserve, enable the option to preserve quotes inside unquoted fields.

6. **Map the Parsed Data to Checkbox Fields**: After parsing the CSV, you will need to map the parsed data to the checkbox fields in Workfront. This typically involves setting up a mapping that aligns the parsed values with the checkbox options available in your Workfront instance.

7. **Test the Configuration**: Once you have set up the parsing and mapping, run a test to ensure that the data is being parsed correctly and that the checkbox fields are being populated as expected.

By following these steps, you should be able to successfully parse a CSV file and populate checkbox fields in Workfront.

message: why do dates go gray when changing
answer:
In Workfront, when you change the date option for work items, the dates may appear gray to indicate that they are not adjustable. This typically happens because the dates for work items that are already on the iteration are not automatically adjusted when you modify the date settings. 

The gray color signifies that those dates are locked or set based on the current iteration settings. If teams are assigning work items to each other's iterations, the behavior can vary: for instance, if Team A modifies work item dates to align with their iteration dates, and Team B does not, the dates will change when Team B assigns a work item to Team A's iteration. However, if Team A assigns a work item to Team B's iteration, the dates will remain unchanged.

If you need to adjust the dates for specific work items, you may need to do so manually after changing the date options.

Sources:

message: what request should I use for a test environment?

1. **Define Your Test Objectives**: Determine what you want to test in your environment. This could include testing workflows, integrations, or user permissions.

2. **Create a Test Project**: Start by creating a project specifically for testing purposes. This project should include all the necessary components that reflect your actual projects.

3. **Use Sample Data**: Populate your test project with sample data that resembles real data. This could include tasks, deadlines, and resources that you would typically use in your live environment.

4. **Simulate Requests**: Create requests that simulate real user actions. For example, if you want to test task assignments, create requests that assign tasks to different users and check how the system handles these assignments.

5. **Monitor and Evaluate**: As you run your tests, monitor the outcomes closely. Evaluate whether the system behaves as expected and document any discrepancies.

6. **Iterate Based on Findings**: Use the results from your testing to make necessary adjustments. This may involve refining your requests or modifying project settings.

By following these steps, you can effectively utilize a test environment in Workfront to ensure that your processes are functioning correctly before going live.

message: Can you limit who can see resource allocation?

3. **Set Project Permissions**: For each project, you can define who has access to view resource allocations. Go to the project settings and adjust the permissions to restrict visibility to only those users or roles that should have access.

4. **Utilize Role View**: In the Resource Planner, remember that you can only see roles associated with projects you can manage. This means that if a user does not have management permissions for a project, they will not see the resource allocations for that project.

message: How do I do my timesheets?
answer:
To complete your timesheets in Workfront, follow these steps:

1. **Access Your Timesheet**: Navigate to the timesheet section within Workfront. This is typically found in the main menu.

2. **Understand the Layout**: Familiarize yourself with the timesheet layout, which includes:
   - **Timesheet Header**: Displays your name and the current time period.
   - **Left Panel**: Contains updates and relevant information.
   - **Work Items**: Lists the tasks or projects you are logging time against.
   - **Toolbar**: Provides options for actions you can take.
   - **Timesheet Footer**: Shows totals and other summary information.
   - **Hour Type**: Select the type of hours you are logging (e.g., regular, overtime).
   - **Hour Entry Comments**: Add any comments related to the hours logged.
   - **Time Frame and Hour-Entry Area**: Where you will enter the hours worked.

3. **Log Your Time**: In the hour-entry area, input the number of hours worked for each task or project. Make sure to select the correct hour type and add any necessary comments.

4. **Review Your Entries**: Check the totals and ensure that all entries are accurate. You can also view and manage comments related to your timesheet.

5. **Submit for Approval**: Once you have completed your entries, submit your timesheet for approval. This is usually done through a button or option in the toolbar.

6. **Approval Process**: After submission, your timesheet will go through an approval process. You can track the status of your submission.

7. **Make Edits if Necessary**: If you need to make changes after submission, you may be able to edit your timesheet depending on your organization's settings.

By following these steps, you can effectively complete and manage your timesheets in Workfront.

message: How do I open up a job number?
answer:
To open up a job number in Workfront, you typically need to follow these steps:

1. **Access the Main Menu**: Click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner of Adobe Workfront.

2. **Navigate to the Relevant Section**: Depending on your organization's setup, you may need to go to a specific area such as Projects or Tasks where job numbers are associated.

3. **Locate the Job Number**: Use the search functionality or browse through the list of projects or tasks to find the job number you want to open.

4. **Open the Job Number**: Click on the job number to view its details. This will allow you to see all associated information, including tasks, resources, and timelines.

If you are unable to find the job number or if it is not visible, ensure that you have the necessary permissions to view it, or consult with your Workfront administrator for assistance.

message: How do I copy dashboards and customize them for me
answer:
To copy dashboards in Workfront and customize them for your needs, follow these steps:

1. Click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner of Workfront, or if available, click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-left corner, then select **Dashboards**.
2. Choose the dashboard you want to copy and click **Copy**. Alternatively, you can open the dashboard you wish to copy and select **Dashboard Actions** > **Copy**.
3. The Dashboard Copy dialog box will appear, displaying all items on the original dashboard.
4. In the **Dashboard Name** field, enter a new name for your copied dashboard.
5. To copy all items from the existing dashboard, leave **Select All** selected. By default, reports, calendars, or lists from the original dashboard will be included in the new dashboard.
6. If you want to transfer only specific items, deselect the items you do not want to include. For each selected item, you can choose the option **Make a Copy**. This will create a new version of the item on the new dashboard, allowing you to modify it without affecting the original.

For example, if you copy a dashboard named ‘Team B’ and rename it to ‘Team A,’ you can customize reports specific to Team A by making copies of the original reports.

This process allows you to tailor dashboards to better fit your requirements while maintaining the integrity of the original dashboards.

Sources:
- [Copy a dashboard: Copy a dashboard1...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/reporting/dashboards/create-dashboards/copy-dashboard#:~:text=Copy%20a%20dashboard1%20Click%20the%20Main,A%20and%20rename%20it%20later%20toTeam%20A%20Report)

message:
- Moving off of a tool call Percolate where they can extract data as a AWS blob storage and wanted to know more about the kickstart ingestion process (import) and any best practices/points of views on that. 
- They also mentioned that if that's too complicated of a process (blob integration), they could do a one-time pull or upload a flat file
answer:
To facilitate the transition from Percolate to Workfront, understanding the Kick-Start ingestion process is essential. Kick-Starts are highly formatted Excel spreadsheets that allow Adobe Workfront administrators to import data from external sources into Workfront. Here’s a breakdown of the process and some best practices:

1. **Kick-Start Import Process**:
   - Kick-Starts are specifically designed for creating new records in Workfront. They are not intended for updating existing records.
   - To import data, you will need to prepare a Kick-Start template that aligns with the data structure required by Workfront. This involves populating the template with the data you wish to import.

2. **Best Practices for Using Kick-Starts**:
   - **Data Preparation**: Ensure that the data you are importing is clean and formatted correctly according to the Kick-Start template specifications. This will help avoid errors during the import process.
   - **Testing**: Before performing a full import, consider doing a test import with a small subset of your data. This allows you to identify any issues without affecting your entire dataset.
   - **Documentation**: Keep a record of the data fields and formats used in your Kick-Start templates. This will be helpful for future imports or if you need to troubleshoot any issues.

3. **Alternative Options**:
   - If the Kick-Start process seems too complex, you can opt for a one-time pull or upload a flat file. This method may be simpler, especially if you have a smaller dataset or if you are looking to perform a one-time migration.

4. **Exporting Data**: If you need to export existing data from Workfront to use in other systems, you can also utilize Kick-Starts for this purpose. This can be beneficial if you want to analyze or manipulate your data outside of Workfront.

By following these guidelines, you can effectively manage the data import process into Workfront and ensure a smooth transition from your previous tool.

Sources:
- [Kick-Starts Data Importer: DocumentationWorkfrontWorkfron,6b-2202-40d6-bcd0-3ee0c2316b43](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/administration-and-setup/manage-wf/kick-starts/kick-starts-data-importer#:~:text=DocumentationWorkfrontWorkfron,6b-2202-40d6-bcd0-3ee0c2316b43)

message: Can the my work dashboard be changed?

Yes, the "My Work" dashboard in Workfront can be changed. To edit your dashboard, follow these steps:

   - **Name**: Change the name of the dashboard.

4. In the **Available Reports & Calendars** section, start typing the name of a report, calendar, or external page in the search bar. You can then drag and drop the desired item into the layout pane on the right.
5. (Optional) Select a new layout for the dashboard by clicking the corresponding radio button.
6. (Optional) To remove an existing report, mouse over its name and click the trash can icon.
7. (Optional) Change the order of the reports by dragging and dropping them in the layout pane.
8. (Optional) Click **Add External Page** to include an external page in the dashboard, or edit an existing external page by hovering over it and clicking the **Edit** icon.

These steps will allow you to customize your dashboard according to your needs.

message: timecard
answer:
In Workfront, a timecard refers to the record of hours logged for projects, tasks, and issues. When you log time, it indicates how much actual time (in hours) you have spent working on a specific project, task, or issue. This logged time becomes the Actual Hours for that item.

To effectively manage your timecard in Workfront, follow these steps:

1. **Log Time**: When you log time, you will need to specify the Hour Entry Date, which is the date you want the hours to be associated with. For example, if you log hours on September 5 but want them associated with September 1, you would set the Hour Entry Date to September 1.

2. **Visibility**: The Hour Entry Date is visible in hour lists and reports, allowing you to track your logged hours effectively.

3. **Best Practices**: It is recommended to log time on working tasks and issues rather than on parent tasks or projects. This is because time logged on working tasks rolls up to the parent tasks and the project as Actual Hours for those items. Similarly, time logged on issues rolls up to the project as Actual Hours for the project.

For more detailed instructions on how to log time in Workfront, you may want to refer to the specific section on logging time.

Sources:
- [Overview of the Project, Task, and Issue dates in Workfront: Hour Entry DateWhen...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/basics/navigate/definitions-pti-dates#:~:text=Hour%20Entry%20DateWhen%20you%20log%20time%20for,as%20Actual%20Hours%20for%20the%20project)

message: How do I create Deliverables types?
answer:
Creating Deliverable types in Workfront involves a few steps within the platform. Here’s how you can do it:

1. **Navigate to the Admin Area**: Start by logging into your Workfront account and go to the Admin area. This is typically found in the main navigation menu.

2. **Select Deliverable Types**: In the Admin area, look for the section related to Deliverables. This may be labeled as "Deliverable Types" or something similar.

3. **Create a New Deliverable Type**: Once you are in the Deliverable Types section, look for an option to create a new Deliverable type. This is usually a button labeled "New" or "Create."

4. **Fill in the Details**: You will need to provide specific information for the new Deliverable type. This may include the name, description, and any other relevant attributes that define the Deliverable type.

5. **Save Your Changes**: After entering all the necessary information, make sure to save your changes. There should be a "Save" button at the bottom of the form.

6. **Review and Adjust**: Once saved, you can review the new Deliverable type and make any adjustments if necessary.

If you encounter any issues or need further customization options, consider checking the settings or help resources available within Workfront.

message: How can I delegate my approvals
answer:
To delegate your approvals in Workfront, follow these steps:

1. Click the **Main Menu** in the upper-right corner, then select **Home**.
2. If necessary, click **Customize** to add the **My Approvals** widget to your Home area.
3. Locate the My Approvals widget and click on **Delegate My Approvals**.
4. In the Delegate My Approvals section, provide the following information:
   - **Name**: Start typing the name of the user you want to delegate your approvals to, and select their name from the drop-down menu.
   - **Start date**: Choose the date when you want the approvals to start being forwarded. Note that this date must be today or a future date, and forwarding will begin at 12:00 A.M. on that date.
   - **End date**: You can either select a date for approvals to stop being forwarded (which will end at 11:59 P.M. on that date) or select **No end date** to delegate approvals indefinitely.
5. Click **Save** to finalize the delegation.

Keep in mind that if approval delegations are disabled by your Workfront administrator, you will not see the Delegate approvals button.

Sources:
- [Delegate approval request: Delegate approvals from...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/review-and-approve-work/work-approvals/delegate-approval-requests#:~:text=Delegate%20approvals%20from%20the%20Home,indefinitely5%20Click%20Save)

message: how do i access training
answer:
To access training in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. **Visit the Adobe Experience League**: This is the primary platform for accessing training resources, tutorials, and documentation related to Workfront.

2. **Search for Workfront Training**: Use the search feature to find specific training modules or resources related to Workfront. You can look for beginner courses, advanced training, or specific features you want to learn about.

3. **Enroll in Courses**: Once you find the relevant training, you can enroll in courses that suit your needs. These may include live sessions, recorded webinars, or self-paced learning modules.

4. **Utilize the AI Assistant**: If you have specific questions or need guidance on certain features, you can use the AI Assistant within Workfront. Click the **AI Assistant** icon near the upper-right corner of the screen and enter your prompt to get information or instructions.

5. **Check for Additional Resources**: Look for any additional resources or community forums where you can ask questions and share knowledge with other Workfront users.

By following these steps, you should be able to access the training you need to effectively use Workfront.

answer:
To start a new request in Workfront, follow these steps:

1. Select "Requests" from the main menu.
2. Click the "New Request" button.
3. In the request type dropdown menu, you will see up to three request queues. Choose the appropriate request queue that fits your needs.

5. Fill out the relevant fields for your request.
6. Click "Submit" to finalize your request.

This process allows you to create and manage requests efficiently within Workfront, tailored to your organization's workflows.

message: why can't I show all custom forms on this template task report?
answer:
To show all custom forms on your template task report in Workfront, you need to ensure that you are following the correct steps to reference the custom forms. Here’s how you can do it:

1. Begin creating your report as described in the article on creating a custom report.
2. On the **Columns** tab, expand the object type that the custom form is applied to. For example, if you want to display all custom forms associated with a task, expand the **Task** field source.
3. Click on **Category Name** to display all custom forms associated with that object.

If you are still unable to see all custom forms, it could be due to the following reasons:

- The custom forms may not be associated with the specific task template you are using.
- There may be permissions or visibility settings that restrict access to certain custom forms.

Make sure to check these aspects to ensure that all relevant custom forms are available for your report.

message: how can i create a grouping in text mode for custom years
answer:
To create a grouping in text mode for custom years in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. Go to an existing project report, or create a new project report.
2. Click **Report Actions** > **Edit**.
3. From the **Grouping** tab, click **Add Grouping**.
4. Click **Switch to Text Mode**.
5. Remove the text in the **Group by** area.
6. Replace the text with the following code, adjusting the year ranges as needed:

```
group.0.linkedname=direct
group.0.name=Custom Year Grouping
group.0.valueexpression=IF(YEAR({entryDate})=YEAR($$TODAY),"Current Year",IF(YEAR({entryDate})=YEAR($$TODAY)-1,"Last Year","Older Years"))
group.0.valueformat=atDateAsYearString
textmode=true
```

7. Click **Done** > **Save Grouping**.
8. (Optional) Update the name of the grouping, then click **Save Grouping**.

This code will group projects based on their entry date into "Current Year," "Last Year," and "Older Years." You can modify the `valueexpression` to create different year groupings as per your requirements.

Sources:
- [Grouping: Projects by Entry Date: Group projects by...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/reporting/reports/custom-view-samples/grouping-projects-entry-date#:~:text=Group%20projects%20by%20Entry%20DateTo,6b-2202-40d6-bcd0-3ee0c2316b43)

message: how about a grouping on custom dates
answer:
To create a grouping in text mode for custom dates in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. Go to an existing project report, or create a new project report.
2. Click **Report Actions** > **Edit**.
3. From the **Grouping** tab, click **Add Grouping**.
4. Click **Switch to Text Mode**.
5. Remove the text in the **Group by** area.
6. Replace the text with the following code, adjusting the date ranges as needed:

```
group.0.linkedname=direct
group.0.name=Custom Date Grouping
group.0.valueexpression=IF({entryDate} >= DATE(2023, 1, 1) && {entryDate} <= DATE(2023, 12, 31), "2023 Dates", IF({entryDate} < DATE(2023, 1, 1), "Before 2023", "After 2023"))
group.0.valueformat=atDateAsDateString
textmode=true
```

7. Click **Done** > **Save Grouping**.
8. (Optional) Update the name of the grouping, then click **Save Grouping**.

This code will group projects based on their entry date into "2023 Dates," "Before 2023," and "After 2023." You can modify the `valueexpression` to create different date groupings as per your requirements.

Sources:
- [Grouping: Projects by Entry Date: Group projects by...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/reporting/reports/custom-view-samples/grouping-projects-entry-date#:~:text=Group%20projects%20by%20Entry%20DateTo,6b-2202-40d6-bcd0-3ee0c2316b43)

message: I'm trying to get a webhook to trigger based on the planned completion date of a task. how do I make that happen in fusion?
answer:
To set up a webhook that triggers based on the planned completion date of a task in Workfront Fusion, you can follow these steps:

1. **Create a New Scenario**: Start by creating a new scenario in Workfront Fusion where you will configure the webhook.

2. **Add a Webhook Module**: Include the "Webhook" module in your scenario. This module will allow you to receive notifications when specific events occur.

3. **Configure the Webhook**: Set up the webhook to listen for changes in tasks. You will need to specify the event that you want to trigger the webhook. In this case, you want to monitor changes related to the planned completion date.

4. **Set Up a Trigger**: Use the "Update a Record" module to monitor the task records. You will need to configure this module to check for updates specifically to the planned completion date field.

5. **Define the Conditions**: In the configuration of the "Update a Record" module, set conditions that specify when the webhook should trigger. For example, you can set it to trigger when the planned completion date is updated or reaches a certain value.

6. **Test the Webhook**: After setting up the webhook and conditions, save your scenario and run a test to ensure that the webhook triggers correctly when the planned completion date of a task changes.

7. **Monitor and Adjust**: Once the webhook is active, monitor its performance and make any necessary adjustments to the conditions or settings to ensure it meets your needs.

By following these steps, you can successfully set up a webhook in Workfront Fusion that triggers based on the planned completion date of a task.

To use the "Watch Events" module specifically for Adobe Workfront, follow these steps:

1. **Create a New Scenario**: Start by creating a new scenario in Workfront Fusion where you will configure the "Watch Events" module.

2. **Add the "Watch Events" Module**: Include the "Watch Events" module in your scenario. This module will allow you to monitor specific events related to your Workfront projects.

3. **Select the Event Type**: In the configuration of the module, you will need to specify which events you want to watch. Common events include file updates, file version updates, file deletions, library publishes, and file comments.

4. **Configure the Webhook**: You will need to select or create a webhook that the module will watch. To add a new webhook, click **Add** next to the Webhook field, enter a name, select the connection you want to use, and specify the event type you want to monitor.

5. **Enter Team ID**: Provide the ID of the team whose events you want the webhook to watch. This ensures that you are monitoring the correct team’s activities.

6. **Set Webhook Status**: Choose whether you want the webhook to be active or paused. This setting will determine if the webhook is currently monitoring events.

7. **Add a Description**: Enter a description for the webhook to help identify its purpose later.

8. **Save Your Configuration**: After completing the setup, click **Save** to save the webhook and return to the module.

9. **Test the Scenario**: Once everything is configured, run a test to ensure that the "Watch Events" module is correctly monitoring the specified events.

By following these steps, you can effectively set up the "Watch Events" module in Workfront Fusion to monitor the events that are most relevant to your projects.

Restricted data in Workfront typically includes information that you do not have permission to access based on your user role, permissions, or access settings. Examples of restricted data might include:

1. **Confidential Projects**: Projects marked as confidential that you do not have access to view.
2. **Private Tasks**: Tasks assigned to other users that are not shared with you.
3. **Sensitive Documents**: Documents that are restricted to certain user groups or roles.
4. **Financial Information**: Budget or financial details that are only accessible to specific roles, such as finance managers.
5. **HR Data**: Employee performance reviews or other HR-related information that is restricted to HR personnel.

message: I am the system administrator. What can I do to find the settings that you will need to know to answer my question?
answer:
As a system administrator in Workfront, you will need to ensure that you have the appropriate access and settings configured to effectively manage user permissions and settings. Here are the key areas to focus on:

1. **Access Level**: Confirm that you have the System Administrator access level. This level grants you full administrative access to manage user settings and configurations.

2. **User Settings**: Within your access level, ensure that the "Edit" option is selected for the Users setting. This allows you to make changes to user accounts.

3. **Fine-tune Settings**: Under the Users setting, check that the "Create" option and at least one of the two User Admin options are enabled. This is crucial for managing user accounts effectively.

4. **Group Administration**: If you are using the User Admin (Group Users) option, verify that you are a group administrator of any group where the user is a member. This will allow you to manage users within that group.

If you encounter any restrictions or if you are unsure about your access level, it is advisable to consult with your Workfront administrator to clarify any additional restrictions that may be in place.

message: How do I change user permissions?
answer:
To change user permissions in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. **Navigate to the User Management Section**: Start by accessing the User Management area within your Workfront account. This is typically found in the Admin section.

2. **Select the User**: Find and select the user whose permissions you want to change. You can search for the user by name or email.

3. **Edit User Permissions**: Once you have selected the user, look for an option to edit or manage permissions. This may be labeled as "Edit Permissions" or something similar.

4. **Adjust Permissions**: In the permissions settings, you can modify the user's access levels. This may include changing their role, adjusting project access, or setting specific permissions for tasks and documents.

5. **Save Changes**: After making the necessary adjustments, ensure you save the changes to apply the new permissions.

6. **Verify Changes**: It’s a good practice to verify that the changes have been applied correctly by checking the user’s permissions again.

If you encounter any issues or need further assistance, consider reaching out to your Workfront administrator or support team for help.

Sources:
- [FTP modules: Actions Change permissions...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront-fusion/using/references/apps-and-their-modules/universal-connectors/ftp-modules#:~:text=Actions%20Change%20permissions%20Create,%28%28%5Br-%5D%5Bw-%5D%5Bx-%5D%29%7B3%7D%29%5B0-7%5D%7B3%2C4%7D)

message: How do I see when a user was set up?
answer:
To see when a user was set up in Workfront, you can utilize the system audit log. The audit log records various actions taken by users, including when a user account is created. Here’s how you can access this information:

1. Navigate to the Setup area in Workfront.
2. Go to the Preferences section.
3. Look for the Audit Logs section.
4. By default, the log will show data from the last seven days. You can change the filter criteria to view a broader date range if needed.
5. In the audit log, look for the entry that corresponds to the creation of the user account. This entry will provide details such as the date and time of the change, the name of the user who completed the action, and any associated details.

This will allow you to find out when a specific user was set up in the system.

Sources:
- [Understand the System Audit Log: DocumentationWorkfrontUndersta,with the action...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront-learn/tutorials-workfront/administration-and-setup/create-and-manage-users/system-audit-log#:~:text=DocumentationWorkfrontUndersta,with%20the%20action%20The%20IP%20address)

message: How do I view the planned versus actual costs for my projects?
answer:
To view the planned versus actual costs for your projects in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. **Access the Projects Area**: Navigate to the Projects area from the main menu in Workfront.

2. **Select Your Project**: Choose the specific project for which you want to view the costs.

3. **View Cost Information**: Look for sections labeled "Planned Cost" and "Actual Cost." 
   - The **Planned Cost** represents the total estimated costs for the project, which can be viewed for the overall life of the project or for a specified date range (like a week or month).
   - The **Actual Cost** shows the total costs incurred to date for the same project, also viewable for the overall life or a specified date range.

4. **Analyze Budgeted Variance**: You can also check the **Budgeted Variance (for Cost)**, which is calculated as the total Budgeted Cost minus the total Actual Cost. This will help you understand if you are over or under budget. A positive variance is displayed in green, indicating that the Budgeted Cost is greater than the Actual Cost, while a negative variance is shown in red, indicating the opposite.

By following these steps, you can effectively monitor and compare the planned and actual costs for your projects in Workfront.

Sources:
- [View Resource Utilization Information: areas of Workfront...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/manage-resources/resource-utilization/view-utilization-information#:~:text=areas%20of%20Workfront%20For%20information,Cost%20is%20less%20than%20the%20Actual%20Cost)

In Workfront, there isn't a built-in feature that automatically sets a task to "complete" once its due date has passed. However, you can manually update the status of a task to "Complete" if it is overdue. 

To do this, follow these steps:

1. Navigate to the task that you want to update.
2. Open the task details by clicking on the task name.
3. Look for the status field in the task details.
4. Change the status to "Complete."
5. Save your changes.

If you frequently have tasks that need to be marked as complete after their due dates, consider setting up a reminder or a report to help you track overdue tasks, so you can manage them more efficiently.

Sources:
- [Overview of the task Planned Completion Date: Manually set the...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/manage-work/tasks/task-information/task-planned-completion-date#:~:text=Manually%20set%20the%20Planned,keep%20the%20Planned%20Start%20Date%20the%20same)

message: how do I turn off a report daily email notification?
answer:
To turn off a report daily email notification in Workfront, you can follow these steps:

1. Click the **Main Menu** icon in the upper-right corner of Adobe Workfront.
2. Click your user name next to your profile picture, or if available, click your profile picture in the top navigation area and then click **Workfront Profile**.
3. Click the **More** icon next to your name, then select **Edit**.
4. In the **Edit Person** box that appears, navigate to the **Notifications** section.
5. Look for the category related to report notifications. Click on that category to view the specific notification settings.
6. Deselect the check box for the daily email notification you wish to turn off. If there is an option to deactivate all notifications in that category, you can use that as well.

After making your changes, ensure that you save your settings. You should see a label indicating that the notification setting has been **Edited**.

If you continue to receive notifications after making these changes, double-check that you have deselected the correct options and that there are no other settings overriding your preferences.

Sources:
- [Modify your own email notifications: View and modify...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/basics/use-notifications/activate-or-deactivate-your-own-event-notifications#:~:text=View%20and%20modify%20your%20email,setting%20has%20beenmodified)

message: can you change the fields visible in a single project?
answer:
To change the fields visible in a single project in Workfront, you will need to work with your system or group administrator, as they have the ability to customize the fields displayed in the project header. Here are the steps you can follow:

1. **Contact Your Administrator**: Reach out to your system or group administrator to discuss the specific fields you want to change or add in the project header.

2. **Field Customization**: Your administrator can customize the fields that are visible in the project header based on your organization's needs. The default fields include:
   - Project name
   - Percent Complete
   - Project Owner
   - Planned Completion Date and time
   - Condition
   - Status

3. **Review Customization Options**: Discuss with your administrator which fields you would like to see and if there are any additional fields that can be added or modified.

4. **Implementation**: Once the changes are agreed upon, your administrator will implement the customization, and you will be able to see the updated fields in the project header.

If you have specific fields in mind that you want to add or modify, make sure to communicate those clearly to your administrator.

Sources:
- [Edit Projects: Edit a project...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/manage-work/projects/manage-projects/edit-projects#:~:text=Edit%20a%20project%20in%20the%20project%20header,in%20a%20current%20approval%20process)

message: can i bulk assign tasks within a project?
answer:
Yes, you can bulk assign tasks within a project in Workfront. Here’s how to do it:

1. Go to a list of tasks that you want to assign in bulk.
2. Ensure that the **Autosave** option is selected if you are on a list of tasks under a project. Note that you cannot edit tasks in bulk when saving tasks manually on a project.
3. Select several tasks in the tasks list.
4. Click **Edit**. The **Edit Tasks** dialog box will open.
5. In the **Assignments** area, select the **Assignee** box, then start typing the name of a user, job role, or team that you want to assign to all the tasks. 
   - If any of the tasks is already assigned, the resources you indicate here will be added to the tasks instead of replacing the existing resources.
6. (Optional) Select the radio button in the **Task Owner** column to indicate which resource is the primary assignee or the Owner of the task, when you assign more than one resource to the task. This option is not available for teams.
7. (Conditional) Specify the **Allocation %** for each resource assigned to the task if all the tasks you selected have a Duration Type of Effort Driven or Calculated Assignment. This indicates how much of their time these resources should spend on completing the task. This is only available for users and job roles.
   - Alternatively, specify the amount of **Hours** for each resource assigned to the task if all the tasks you selected have a Duration Type of Simple. The total of all hours for all resources should equal the number of Planned Hours for the task.
   - Note that you cannot specify the allocation percentage or the number of hours per resource if the tasks you selected have different Duration Types.

By following these steps, you can efficiently assign multiple tasks to users within a project.

Sources:
- [Assign tasks: Assign multiple tasks...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/manage-work/tasks/assign-tasks/assign-tasks#:~:text=Assign%20multiple%20tasks%20to%20a%20user1%20Go,different%20Duration%20Types%20or%20of%20the)

message: Can I bulk assign tasks by role within a project?
answer:
Yes, you can bulk assign tasks by role within a project in Workfront. Here’s how to do it:

1. Navigate to the list of tasks within the project where you want to make bulk assignments.
2. Ensure that the **Autosave** option is selected, as this is necessary for bulk editing.
3. Select the tasks you wish to assign in bulk.
4. Click on **Edit** to open the **Edit Tasks** dialog box.
5. In the **Assignments** area, you can assign a user to all work items currently assigned to a specific job role. Start typing the name of the job role in the **Assignee** box.
6. If any of the tasks are already assigned, the resources you indicate will be added to the tasks instead of replacing the existing resources.
7. (Optional) If you are assigning multiple resources, you can specify which resource is the primary assignee by selecting the radio button in the **Task Owner** column.
8. (Conditional) If the tasks have a Duration Type of Effort Driven or Calculated Assignment, you can specify the **Allocation %** for each resource. Alternatively, if the tasks have a Duration Type of Simple, you can specify the total **Hours** for each resource.

By following these steps, you can efficiently bulk assign tasks by role within your project.

Sources:
- [Assign Work in Bulk using the Workload Balancer: Considerations for making...](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/manage-resources/the-workload-balancer/assign-work-in-workload-balancer-in-bulk#:~:text=Considerations%20for%20making%20bulk,to%20remove%20Rick%20from%20these%20tasks)

message: text mode to use comma numbers format
answer:
To format numbers with commas in text mode reports in Workfront, you can use the `valueformat` line in your column settings. Here’s how to do it step by step:

1. **Access the Report**: Open the report where you want to format the numbers.
2. **Edit the Column**: Locate the column that contains the numeric values you want to format.
3. **Modify the Value Format**: In the column settings, you will need to set the `valueformat` line to use the appropriate formatting for comma-separated numbers. For example, if you want to display a number like 1,234, you would use:
   ```
   valueformat=doubleAsInt
   ```
   This will format the number with commas as thousands separators.

4. **Save Changes**: After making the changes, ensure you save the report to apply the new formatting.

By following these steps, you can effectively display numbers in a comma-separated format in your Workfront reports.

Sources:
- [Format numbers, currency and percentage values in text mode reports: DocumentationWorkfrontWorkfron,6b-2202-40d6-bcd0-3ee0c2316b43](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront/using/reporting/reports/text-mode/format-numbers-in-text-mode-reports#:~:text=DocumentationWorkfrontWorkfron,6b-2202-40d6-bcd0-3ee0c2316b43)
- [Math functions: formatNumber (number,ad-4d59-4355-88ab-a2e62ed7d1a3](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/workfront-fusion/using/references/mapping-panel/functions/math-functions#:~:text=formatNumber%20%28number,ad-4d59-4355-88ab-a2e62ed7d1a3)
